title: Period of sinusoidal functions from equation
description: Given the formula of a sinusoidal function, determine its period.
topic_path: Period of sinusoidal functions from equation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $f(x)=3\sin(2x-1)+4$?**  
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of $f(x) = 3\sin(\maroonD{2}x-1)+4$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD 2|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \pi \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $f(x)=3\sin(2x-1)+4$ is $\pi$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $g(x)=2\cos(7x+5)+1$?**  
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]
###Finding the period

The period of  $g(x) = 2\cos(\maroonD{7}x+5)+1$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD 7|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{2\pi}{7} \\
 \end{align}$
###The answer

The period of $g(x) = 2\cos({7}x+5)+1$ is $\dfrac{2\pi}{7}$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $h(x)=5\sin(4x-2)-3$?**  
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $h(x) = 5\sin(\maroonD{4}x-2)-3$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD 4|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{\pi}{2} \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $h(x) = 5\sin({4}x-2)-3$ is $ \dfrac{\pi}{2}$ units.

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $f(x) = -4\cos(\maroonD{5}x-9)-7$ is:

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $g(x)=-\sin(-8x-3)+5$?**  
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$   is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $g(x) = -\sin(\maroonD{-8}x-3)+5$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD {-8}|} \\\\\\\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{8}\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{\pi}{4} \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $g(x) = -\sin({-8}x-3)+5$ is $\dfrac{\pi}{4} $ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $h(x)=-3\cos(\pi x+2)-6$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $h(x) = -3\cos(\maroonD{\pi}x+2)-6$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD \pi|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= 2 \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $h(x) = -3\cos({\pi}x+2)-6$ is $2$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $f(x)=-6\sin(3\pi x+4)-2$?**   
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$   is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $f(x) = -6\sin(\maroonD{3\pi}x+4)-2$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD {3\pi}|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{2}{3} \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $f(x) = -6\sin({3\pi}x+4)-2$ is $\dfrac23$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of the function $g(x)=-9\cos\left(-\dfrac{\pi}{2} x-6\right)+8$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $g(x) = -9\cos\left(\maroonD{-\dfrac{\pi}{2}}x-6\right)+8$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{\left|\maroonD{-\dfrac{\pi}{2}}\right |} \\\\\\\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{\dfrac{\pi}{2}}\\\\\\\\
&= 2\pi\cdot \dfrac{2}{\pi} \\\\
&=4
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $g(x) = -9\cos\left({-\dfrac{\pi}{2}}x-6\right)+8$ is $4$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=7\sin\left(-\dfrac{3\pi}{4} x-\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)+6$?**  
*Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y = 7\sin\left(\maroonD{-\dfrac{3\pi}{4}}x-\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)+6$ is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{\left|\maroonD{-\dfrac{3\pi}{4}} \right|} \\\\\\\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{{\dfrac{3\pi}{4}}}\\\\\\\\\\
&= 2\pi\cdot\dfrac{4}{3\pi}\\ \\
&=\dfrac83
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y = 7\sin\left({-\dfrac{3\pi}{4}}x-\dfrac{\pi}{4}\right)+6$ is $\dfrac83$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=8\cos\left(5\pi x+\dfrac{3\pi}{2}\right)-9$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y = 8\cos\left(\maroonD{5\pi}x+\dfrac{3\pi}{2}\right)-9$  is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{5\pi}|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{2}{5} \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y = 8\cos\left({5\pi}x+\dfrac{3\pi}{2}\right)-9$  is $\dfrac25$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=3\cos(x-7)+2$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y = 3\cos(\maroonD{1}x-7)+2$  is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{1}|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= 2\pi \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y = 3\cos(x-7)+2$  is $2\pi$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=-2\sin\left(\dfrac23x-4\right)$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\sin(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\sin(\maroonD {b}x + c) + {d}$ is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y=-2\sin\left(\maroonD{\dfrac23}x-4\right)$  is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{\left|\maroonD{\dfrac23}\right|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= 2\pi\cdot \dfrac{3}{2} \\\\
&=3\pi
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y=-2\sin\left(\dfrac23x-4\right)$  is $3\pi$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=-5\cos\left(\dfrac{\pi}{8} x\right)+3$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y=-5\cos\left(\maroonD{\dfrac{\pi}{8}} x\right)+3$  is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{\left|\maroonD{\dfrac{\pi}8}\right|} \\\\\\\\\\
&=2\pi\cdot \dfrac{8}{\pi}  \\\\
&=16
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y=-5\cos\left(\dfrac{\pi}{8} x\right)+3$  is $16$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the period of $y=\cos(-4\pi x+3)-7$?**  
 *Give an exact value.*

[[☃ expression 1]] units
hints:
###Period in sinusoids of the form $y=a\cos(bx+c)+d$

Graphically, the period of a sinusoidal function is the horizontal distance between the ends of a single cycle of its graph.

The period of a sinusoid of the form $y={a}\cos(\maroonD bx + c) + {d}$  is equal to $\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD b|}$. 

 [[☃ explanation 2]]

###Finding the period

The period of  $y=\cos(\maroonD{-4\pi} x+3)-7$  is:

$\begin{align} \text{period}&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{b}|}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\pi}{|\maroonD{-4\pi}|} \\\\\\\\\\
 &= \dfrac{1}{2} \\
 \end{align}$

###The answer

The period of $y=\cos(-4\pi x+3)-7$  is $\dfrac12$ units.
